mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series rise concurrently, with a win rate below 45% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of observations during late-cycle phases, invalidating the earnings transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across all tested regimes, with a Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth surpassing 2.0% confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain negative or flat in 70% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds the 2.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting household income and equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a positive return win rate below 40% over a 4-year rolling window, indicating decoupling from equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening phases, rising industrial production signals capacity constraints and potential supply bottlenecks that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening cycles produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, contradicting the supply constraint mechanism.
